response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a highly experienced web and full-stack developer, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role on every critical dimension. The role mandates native-level Japanese proficiency — a hard requirement — which is entirely absent from her profile. Her programming background centers on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies, with only peripheral C/C++ exposure from a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project. There is no evidence of Linux kernel development, embedded firmware work, L2/L3 networking experience, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption systems). Her career trajectory is firmly in web platforms, GIS tooling, and developer tooling — orthogonal to embedded networking security.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple languages and paradigms demonstrates general engineering competence
Some C/C++ exposure from National Instruments (NI LabWindows/CVI) and embedded-adjacent work (robotic arms, hardware control at Altom) shows non-zero low-level systems familiarity
Strong DevOps and Linux environment experience (GitLab, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes) indicates comfort working in Unix-based development contexts
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency listed anywhere — this is a stated hard requirement and is an immediate disqualifier
No embedded networking or security domain experience: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, NETCONF, SNMP, or Linux kernel modification work
C programming experience is superficial and dated (2014–2015 NI role and a thesis project); the role demands deep, current C proficiency for embedded UNIX firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security experience
